methodsAttempted.query: site:uic.edu UIC generative AI policy ChatGPT academic integrity teaching privacy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced official UIC IT, UIC Today, CATE, College of Medicine, and law library AI pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uic.edu UIC artificial intelligence guidance students faculty syllabus academic integrity AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found CATE teaching guides, UIC Today faculty guidance, and unrelated course syllabus material.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uic.edu UIC AI tools privacy data security generative AI policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the central IT responsible-use statement, IT AI resources page, and Microsoft 365 Copilot announcement.
Robots.txt checks for www.uic.edu, it.uic.edu, teaching.uic.edu, today.uic.edu, and medicine.uic.edu allowed the crawled public paths; WordPress admin/plugin paths were not crawled.

artifacts.summary: Official UIC discovery found a central Information Technology responsible-use statement, an IT AI resources and data-security page, an official UIC Today faculty guidance communication, a CATE teaching guide on AI writing tools, and a College of Medicine policy for student academic work. Claims are scoped to those source authorities, with the medicine policy kept college-specific.

targets.sourceTitle: Statement on Responsible and Acceptable Use of Generative AI
Generative AI resources and guidance for faculty
Policy on Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Student Academic Work
expectedThemes: College of Medicine student academic work

stopConditions: Stop after official UIC sources provide central and college-scoped claim candidates.
Do not broaden College of Medicine policy into university-wide student policy.
Do not use official news or teaching guides as binding policy unless the source wording imposes a requirement.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Statement on Responsible and Acceptable Use of Generative AI
Generative AI resources and guidance for faculty
Policy on Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Student Academic Work

artifacts.claimText: UIC states that students, staff, faculty, and researchers who need to enter high-risk or sensitive data into a generative AI tool or service should submit a ticket to security@uic.edu, and that high-risk or sensitive data in any generative-AI solution must have appropriate contracts and data-use agreements.

citation.citationTitle: Statement on Responsible and Acceptable Use of Generative AI
citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago Information Technology

artifacts.claimText: UIC IT states that Copilot Chat is protected by enterprise data protection only when a client is signed in with UIC credentials, and that no university data may be shared in the consumer or personal Microsoft Copilot product when clients do not sign in with UIC credentials.

citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago Information Technology

artifacts.claimText: UIC CATE recommends that instructors develop and include an AI usage policy in their syllabus after sharing their stance on AI writing tools in the course.

citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago Center for the Advancement of Teaching Excellence

artifacts.claimText: UIC CATE guidance says students presenting a significant amount of minimally edited AI-generated text as their own should cite the specific AI writing tool, and it warns that AI-generated citations may be real, partially correct, or fabricated.

citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago Center for the Advancement of Teaching Excellence

artifacts.claimText: UIC College of Medicine policy states that students are expected to draft submitted assignments on their own; AI may assist with idea generation and editing, but AI directly authoring a response is considered a violation of academic integrity and professionalism.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Student Academic Work
citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago College of Medicine

artifacts.claimText: UIC College of Medicine policy prohibits students from generating patient care notes with AI applications except EHR-supported applications explicitly permitted by course leadership, and says protected health information should never be entered into outside generative AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Student Academic Work
citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago College of Medicine

artifacts.sourceTitle: Statement on Responsible and Acceptable Use of Generative AI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students, staff, faculty, and researchers who need to enter high-risk or sensitive data into a generative AI tool or service should submit a ticket to security@uic.edu and reference the above guidance, principles, and policies. High-risk or sensitive data created, stored, or managed in any generative-AI solution must have appropriate contracts and data-use agreements in place.

citation.citationTitle: Statement on Responsible and Acceptable Use of Generative AI
citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago Information Technology

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat: This is the free Copilot chat. Clients must sign in with their UIC credentials to protect data. This is also protected by EDP only when a client is signed in. Microsoft Copilot: This is Copilot chat if clients do not sign-in with UIC credentials. This is the consumer/personal version and is not protected by EDP. No university data may be shared in this product.

citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago Information Technology

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Share with students your personal stance on AI writing tools in the classroom. Defining your own thoughts on these tools brings transparency to the issue and clarifies the ethical implication of their usage in the classroom. Develop and include an AI usage policy in your syllabus.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: How to Approach AI Writing Tools in Your Classroom

citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago Center for the Advancement of Teaching Excellence

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Please note that you need to cite the specific AI writing tool as a source if you present any significant amount (i.e., more than one sentence) of minimally edited AI-generated text as your own. Users should expect generative AI writing tools to produce a mix of real, partially correct, and completely fabricated citations.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Example statement permitting limited use of AI writing tools; Evaluating Citations Generated by AI Writing Tools

citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago Center for the Advancement of Teaching Excellence

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Student Academic Work
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Submitted assignments are expected to be written by students. While AI may be used to assist in idea generation and editing, students are expected to draft their assignment on their own. Use of AI to directly author the response is considered a violation of academic integrity and professionalism.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Student Academic Work
citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago College of Medicine

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Student Academic Work
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students are prohibited from generating patient care notes using AI applications other than those supported by the electronic health record (EHR), such as predictive text, and only if explicitly permitted by course leadership. Protected health information should never be entered into a generative AI tool outside of one supported and authorized by the EHR within the patient’s healthcare facility.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Student Academic Work
citation.publisher: University of Illinois Chicago College of Medicine

artifacts.notes: Claim wording follows the source should/must distinction and is scoped to UIC's central IT statement.
Claim is limited to the Copilot data-protection wording on the official IT AI Resources page.
Claim uses recommends rather than must because the source is teaching guidance with example policy language.
Claim combines adjacent CATE guidance on citation and verification without turning examples into binding university-wide policy.
Claim is explicitly scoped to the College of Medicine and mirrors policy language about direct AI authorship.
Claim is college-scoped and limited to the PHI and EHR-supported AI-applications language in the policy.

artifacts.title: University of Illinois Chicago (UIC) AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official UIC sources provide central responsible-use and sensitive-data guidance through Information Technology, tool-specific data-security guidance through the IT AI Resources page, teaching guidance through CATE, and a College of Medicine AI policy for student academic work. The strongest university-level evidence concerns responsible AI use, high-risk or sensitive data, and UIC-credential requirements for protected Copilot use. College of Medicine claims are not broadened to all UIC students.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The College of Medicine policy applies to that college's student academic work and should not be read as a university-wide student AI policy without additional central UIC evidence.
UIC Today and CATE guidance sources are used for guidance claims, not as binding policy unless their text directly says so.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
